agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 zoning bylaw with 2026 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal Burnaby's current Zoning Bylaw (from 1965) and replace it with a new Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed change would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, the new rules would affect what you can build or do on your property, including new requirements for permits.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak up about how the new rules would affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority seeks Year 2 operating funds
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) for Year 2 operating funding. The BHA is a city-owned corporation that builds and manages affordable housing. If approved, this funding would support the BHA's day-to-day operations for its second year of running. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on this request at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this funding could affect future housing projects and property development in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on the proposed 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) properties. If approved, this would set the tax rates that commercial and residential property owners in Burnaby will pay in 2026. The decision will affect how much you owe in property taxes next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the legal authority to approve or reject plans for new subdivisions (splitting a piece of land into smaller lots) and road closures. This decision is about who will hold that power in Burnaby. The appointment has not been made yet — Council will decide at the upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision — it could affect future subdivision applications.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the city's zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current rules allow. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider and vote on this request at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variances in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on insurance contract renewals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. This is a routine renewal of insurance coverage that protects city assets and operations. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming meeting and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission trip to 2026 BC conference
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a request for members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. If approved, commission members would travel to the conference to learn about heritage conservation and network with other communities. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine procurement decision that keeps city technology systems working, but it does not directly create new rules or fees for local businesses.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on furniture contract for new civic facility
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for the new civic facility. If approved, this contract would furnish the new building. The decision is not final — it will be voted on at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the city's contract for external audit services. This is not a final decision yet. If approved, the change would modify the existing agreement with the company that audits the city's financial records. The purpose is to ensure the city's finances are reviewed properly. The specific details of the change are on the council agenda for discussion and a vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex geotechnical work
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract change (an amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consultant services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to make sure buildings are safe and stable. This proposed change would adjust the scope or cost of the current consulting contract. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant intake. This decision will determine which local organizations and projects receive funding from the city. The grants are part of the city's regular program to support community groups, and council will decide whether to accept the staff recommendations as presented.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would update certain fees the city charges for various services and permits. The changes are not yet approved — council will vote on them at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, these fee changes could affect your business licence or permit costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and Framework. If approved, this bylaw would create new rules for landlords, including limits on rent increases above the provincial guideline and stronger protections against evictions for renovations. The goal is to help keep tenants in their homes and slow down displacement. Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting — it has not been passed yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider attending the public hearing to voice your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway Upgrade improvements
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety upgrades along the route. This is a proposed decision — it has not been finalized yet. The project aims to make it safer and easier for people to bike through southeast Burnaby. Business owners along or near the route should be aware that construction could affect traffic, parking, and customer access if the plan is approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on traffic and parking near your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design standards for safer intersections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new intersections to use 'protected intersection geometry' — a safer design that separates pedestrians and cyclists from turning vehicles using curbs and barriers. This is meant to make walking and biking safer for people of all ages and abilities. The decision has not been made yet; Council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if approved, this will affect how new developments in Burnaby's town centre are designed.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby staff recommend against exempting Brentwood Park from SSMUH rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will receive a staff report on April 28, 2026, recommending against exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) requirements. The report states the City is bound by provincial law to zone for SSMUH and that Restrictive Covenants are not a recognized exemption. A proposed Building Bylaw amendment to require compliance with Restrictive Covenants would apply city-wide to all property types, adding application costs and processing time, and is not recommended.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's decision on April 28, 2026 — if you own property in Brentwood Park with a Restrictive Covenant, the exemption you hoped for may not proceed.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council considers Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption report

agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

attachments.title: Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This is an information-only item — no vote or decision is required. The report shows how much construction activity is happening in the city, which can affect local business conditions, traffic, and demand for services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage status for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue under review
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider an 'as-found report' and 'Statement of Significance' for the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. A Statement of Significance is a formal document that explains why a building or site is historically important. If Council later votes to designate this property as a heritage resource, it could affect what renovations or changes the owner can make. This item is on the agenda for information only — no final decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the next council meeting — if a heritage designation bylaw is proposed, you may need to attend a public hearing to have your say.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the City of Burnaby's 2025 annual report on solid waste and recycling collection. This report summarizes how the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection system performed over the past year. It is a proposed review of the system's effectiveness, not a final decision. Council may use this report to guide future changes to waste and recycling services.
agenda_briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on proposed food sustainability measures in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an operational update on proposed food sustainability measures. This update will inform Council about current programs and potential future actions to improve food security and reduce waste in the city. No final decisions have been made yet, but this report could lead to new policies or programs that affect local food businesses and community gardens.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Safety improvements at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to make the intersection safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming item on the council agenda for discussion and a potential vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on rezoning for hotel, more homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would change the approved development at 3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Road, and 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place. The original plan allowed two residential towers (80 and 73 storeys) with a 13-storey retail and office podium. The new proposal would replace some office space with a hotel and restaurant, add 362 more market strata units (condos you can buy), and make Tower 1B seven storeys taller and Tower 1A four storeys taller. The developer also wants to move the required affordable housing for Phase 1 to a different site (the Carrigan site). This is not final — council will consider and vote on the change.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone 6535 Kingsway. The plan would allow a 12-storey building with 290 residential units, ground-floor shops facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This proposal matches the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw amendment to rezone 2757 Norland Avenue. If approved, the change would allow construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal follows the 1998 Official Community Plan and approved transition rules. This is not yet a done deal — the decision is up for a vote at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and attend to voice your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming proposal on the council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this pilot could affect how your business operates if you use or sell e-bikes or e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — the decision is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, the pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes or e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for violations related to these devices. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect how e-bike and e-scooter rules are enforced in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates for all land and buildings
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would set the 2026 tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in Burnaby. This bylaw, called the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026, is required under sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter (the main law that gives cities their powers). If approved, it will determine how much property tax you pay next year based on the assessed value of your land and any improvements (like buildings or renovations).
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
